title: Getting started with endogCopula
text: Endogeneity arises whenever regressor correlated error term regression model, example omitted variables, simultaneity, measurement error. Ordinary least squares biased inconsistent. classical remedy instrumental variable, valid strong instruments notoriously hard find. Park Gupta (2012) proposed instrument-free alternative: model joint distribution endogenous regressor structural error Gaussian copula. assumption endogenous regressor non-normally distributed error normal, dependence two can recovered data , adding “copula control function” – transformation endogenous regressor’s estimated cumulative distribution function (CDF) – regression removes endogeneity bias. lively methodological literature refined idea. Becker, Proksch, Ringle (2022) revisited original approach highlighted conditions works well, Qian, Koschmann, Xie (2024) provide practical guide. Newer estimators address correlation endogenous exogenous regressors, original approach ignores: Yang, Qian, Xie (2025) introduce two-stage copula generated regressor approach (2sCOPE), Hu, Qian, Xie (2025) develop nonparametric two-stage variant (2sCOPE-np), Breitung, Mayer, Wied (2024) study corrections based nonlinear transformations, Haschka (2024) exploits -regressor correlation, Liengaard et al. (2025) propose adjusted estimator improved finite-sample behaviour. endogCopula package collects cross-sectional estimators behind single formula interface provides bootstrap standard errors plus print(), summary(), coef(), residuals(), confint() methods. implementations tested numerical equivalence published replication code accompanying papers.

title: Getting started with endogCopula
text: cross-sectional estimators use two-part formula, variables bar continuous endogenous regressors variables bar exogenous. Add - 1 drop intercept wrap dummies .factor().
code: y ~ endog1 + endog2 | exog1 + exog2

title: Getting started with endogCopula
text: package ships simulated dataset, sim_endog, generated y=1+2zendog+1.5xexog+u,y = 1 + 2\, z_{\text{endog}} + 1.5\, x_{\text{exog}} + u, latent Gaussian factor makes z_endog correlated structural error u, w_instr shifts z_endog without entering outcome equation. naive OLS regression ignores endogeneity z_endog produces biased estimate coefficient (true value: 2): Park Gupta (2012) estimator adds copula control function z_endog. use nboots = 25 vignette builds quickly; practice several hundred bootstrap replicates recommended. 2sCOPE estimator Yang, Qian, Xie (2025) additionally accounts correlation endogenous exogenous regressors: Comparing estimates endogenous coefficient: Confidence intervals based bootstrap replicates available via confint():
code: library(endogCopula) data(sim_endog) str(sim_endog) #> 'data.frame':    2000 obs. of  4 variables: #>  $ y      : num  0.0945 4.2063 -2.7204 3.1972 2.1352 ... #>  $ z_endog: num  0.3616 0.8219 -1.5403 1.3684 -0.0897 ... #>  $ x_exog : num  -0.699 0.996 -0.693 -0.103 0.604 ... #>  $ w_instr: num  1.482 1.705 -0.934 0.605 -0.524 ... ols <- lm(y ~ z_endog + x_exog, data = sim_endog) coef(ols) #> (Intercept)     z_endog      x_exog  #>   0.9826393   2.0704182   1.5140852 set.seed(42) fit_pg <- CopRegPG(   y ~ z_endog | x_exog + w_instr,   data = sim_endog,   cdf = "resc.ecdf",   nboots = 25 ) summary(fit_pg) #> Gaussian copula estimator: Park and Gupta (2012) #> CDF transformation: resc.ecdf #>  #> Call: #> CopRegPG(formula = y ~ z_endog | x_exog + w_instr, data = sim_endog,  #>     cdf = "resc.ecdf", nboots = 25) #>  #> Coefficients: #>                Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)     #> `(Intercept)`  1.004580   0.035742 28.1065  < 2e-16 *** #> z_endog        1.155322   0.563230  2.0512  0.04024 *   #> x_exog         1.513418   0.018161 83.3311  < 2e-16 *** #> w_instr       -0.053370   0.024242 -2.2015  0.02770 *   #> z_endog_cop    1.024409   0.607497  1.6863  0.09174 .   #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #>  #> Standard errors based on 25 bootstrap replicates. set.seed(42) fit_2scope <- CopReg2sCOPE(   y ~ z_endog | x_exog + w_instr,   data = sim_endog,   cdf = "resc.ecdf",   nboots = 25 ) summary(fit_2scope) #> Gaussian copula estimator: Yang et al. (2025) #> CDF transformation: resc.ecdf #>  #> Call: #> CopReg2sCOPE(formula = y ~ z_endog | x_exog + w_instr, data = sim_endog,  #>     cdf = "resc.ecdf", nboots = 25) #>  #> Coefficients: #>               Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)     #> `(Intercept)` 0.990964   0.029324 33.7933  < 2e-16 *** #> z_endog       1.434730   0.562956  2.5486  0.01082 *   #> x_exog        1.513242   0.022765 66.4718  < 2e-16 *** #> w_instr       0.334912   0.329181  1.0174  0.30896     #> z_endog_cop   0.720331   0.599489  1.2016  0.22953     #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #>  #> Standard errors based on 25 bootstrap replicates. c(   OLS      = unname(coef(ols)["z_endog"]),   PG       = unname(coef(fit_pg)["z_endog"]),   `2sCOPE` = unname(coef(fit_2scope)["z_endog"]) ) #>      OLS       PG   2sCOPE  #> 2.070418 1.155322 1.434730 confint(fit_pg, parm = "z_endog") #>              2.5 %   97.5 % #> z_endog 0.05141198 2.259232 confint(fit_pg, parm = "z_endog", type = "percentile") #>             2.5 %   97.5 % #> z_endog 0.1874727 2.303848

title: Getting started with endogCopula
text: copula control function depends estimate endogenous regressor’s marginal CDF, selected via cdf argument: "kde" – integral kernel density estimator, used Park Gupta (2012) Haschka (2022). Requires suggested ks package. "ecdf" – empirical CDF boundary values replaced, proposed Becker, Proksch, Ringle (2022). "resc.ecdf" – rescaled empirical CDF proposed Qian, Koschmann, Xie (2024) used Haschka (2024) Yang, Qian, Xie (2025). "adj.ecdf" – adjusted empirical CDF proposed Liengaard et al. (2025). pragmatic default use CDF estimator paper introduced estimator applying (e.g. "adj.ecdf" CopRegJAMS(), "resc.ecdf" CopReg2sCOPE()), check conclusions robust across alternatives.

title: Getting started with endogCopula
text: fixed-effects panel estimator Haschka (2022) Bayesian sampler Haschka (2022b) live dedicated companion packages: endogCopulaPanel CopRegML_par(), maximum likelihood panel estimator. endogCopulaBayes CopRegBayes(), MCMC sampler. packages installed, wrappers names exported endogCopula delegate .

title: Getting started with endogCopula
text: Becker, J.-M., D. Proksch, C. M. Ringle (2022). Revisiting Gaussian copulas handle endogenous regressors. Journal Academy Marketing Science 50, 46–66. Breitung, J., . Mayer, D. Wied (2024). Asymptotic properties endogeneity corrections using nonlinear transformations. Econometrics Journal 27(3), 362–383. Haschka, R. E. (2022). Handling endogenous regressors using copulas: generalisation linear panel models fixed effects correlated regressors. Journal Marketing Research 59(4), 860–881. Haschka, R. E. (2022b). Bayesian inference joint estimation models using copulas handle endogenous regressors. https://ssrn.com/abstract=4235194 Haschka, R. E. (2024). Robustness copula-correction models causal analysis: Exploiting -regressor correlation. IMA Journal Management Mathematics 36(1), 161–180. Hu, X., Y. Qian, H. Xie (2025). Correcting endogeneity via instrument-free two-stage nonparametric copula control functions. NBER Working Paper 33607. http://www.nber.org/papers/w33607 Liengaard, B. D., J.-M. Becker, M. Bennedsen, P. Heiler, L. N. Taylor, C. M. Ringle (2025). Dealing regression models’ endogeneity means adjusted estimator Gaussian copula approach. Journal Academy Marketing Science 53, 279–299. Park, S. S. Gupta (2012). Handling endogenous regressors joint estimation using copulas. Marketing Science 31(4), 567–586. Qian, Y., . Koschmann, H. Xie (2024). practical guide endogeneity correction using copulas. NBER Working Paper 32231. https://www.nber.org/papers/w32231 Yang, F., Y. Qian, H. Xie (2025). Addressing endogeneity using two-stage copula generated regressor approach. Journal Marketing Research 62(4), 601–623.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Ashwin Malshe. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Malshe (2026). endogCopula: Endogeneity Corrections via Gaussian Copulas. R package version 0.1.0, https://ashgreat.github.io/endogCopula/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {endogCopula: Endogeneity Corrections via Gaussian Copulas},   author = {Ashwin Malshe},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://ashgreat.github.io/endogCopula/}, }

title: Endogeneity Corrections via Gaussian Copulas
text: Package website: https://ashgreat.github.io/endogCopula/ endogCopula provides instrument-free, Gaussian copula based corrections endogenous regressors linear models. unifies six recently published cross-sectional estimators – Park Gupta (2012), Yang et al. (2025), Hu et al. (2025), Breitung et al. (2024), Haschka (2024), Liengaard et al. (2025) – behind single two-part formula interface shared input validation, choice marginal CDF estimators, bootstrap standard errors, print(), summary(), coef(), residuals(), confint() methods.

title: Endogeneity Corrections via Gaussian Copulas
text: Install development version GitHub remotes pak:
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("ashgreat/endogCopula")  # or # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("ashgreat/endogCopula")

title: Endogeneity Corrections via Gaussian Copulas
text: formula reads y ~ endogenous | exogenous: variables bar treated continuous endogenous regressors, variables bar exogenous. Use - 1 drop intercept .factor() dummies. cdf argument selects marginal CDF estimator ("kde", "ecdf", "resc.ecdf", "adj.ecdf"); see vignette("endogCopula") guidance.
code: library(endogCopula) data(sim_endog)  # Naive OLS is biased for the endogenous regressor (true coefficient: 2) ols <- lm(y ~ z_endog + x_exog, data = sim_endog) coef(ols)["z_endog"]  # Park and Gupta (2012) copula correction fit <- CopRegPG(   y ~ z_endog | x_exog + w_instr,   data = sim_endog,   cdf = "resc.ecdf",   nboots = 199 ) summary(fit) confint(fit)

what: Numerical equivalence with the published reference code
title: Endogeneity Corrections via Gaussian Copulas
text: estimator implementations ports replication code published alongside papers (“Copula-based endogeneity corrections”). package test suite fits estimator corresponding reference functions data asserts numerical equivalence resulting coefficient estimates, refactoring silently change statistical results.

title: Endogeneity Corrections via Gaussian Copulas
text: panel Bayesian estimators live dedicated packages: endogCopulaPanel – fixed-effects copula panel model Haschka (2022), exported CopRegML_par(). endogCopulaBayes – Bayesian sampler Haschka (2022b), exported CopRegBayes(). packages installed, wrappers names endogCopula delegate .

title: Endogeneity Corrections via Gaussian Copulas
text: vignette("endogCopula") – getting started, worked example, guidance choosing cdf argument. Help pages individual estimators (e.g. ?CopRegJAMS) assumptions, references, runnable examples.

title: Endogeneity Corrections via Gaussian Copulas
text: MIT © Ashwin Malshe

what: Copula Regression: Yang et al. (2025) — CopReg2sCOPE
title: Copula Regression: Yang et al. (2025) — CopReg2sCOPE
text: Implements two-stage copula generated regressor estimator proposed Yang et al. (2025).

title: Copula Regression: Yang et al. (2025) — CopReg2sCOPE
code: CopReg2sCOPE(   formula,   data,   cdf = c("kde", "ecdf", "resc.ecdf", "adj.ecdf"),   nboots = 199 )

title: Copula Regression: Yang et al. (2025) — CopReg2sCOPE
text: formula two-part formula form y ~ endog1 + endog2 | exog1 + exog2, left-hand side lists endogenous regressors right-hand side bar lists exogenous regressors. Use - 1 remove intercept. data data.frame containing variables referenced formula. cdf Character string specifying cumulative distribution function estimator. One "kde", "ecdf", "resc.ecdf", "adj.ecdf". nboots Number bootstrap replications used compute standard errors. Set zero skip bootstrapping.

title: Copula Regression: Yang et al. (2025) — CopReg2sCOPE
text: object class endog_copula_fit: list components call matched call. method Character string identifying estimator. cdf CDF estimator used copula transformation. coefficients Matrix coefficient estimates Estimate column , nboots > 0, Std. Error column computed bootstrap replicates. residuals Numeric vector residuals structural part model (copula control terms excluded). bootstrap Matrix bootstrap coefficient replicates, NULL nboots = 0. Objects class support print(), summary(), coef(), residuals(), confint().

title: Copula Regression: Yang et al. (2025) — CopReg2sCOPE
text: Yang, F., Y. Qian, H. Xie (2025). Addressing endogeneity using two-stage copula generated regressor approach. Journal Marketing Research 62(4), 601–623.

title: Copula Regression: Yang et al. (2025) — CopReg2sCOPE
code: data(sim_endog)  fit <- CopReg2sCOPE(y ~ z_endog | x_exog + w_instr, data = sim_endog,                     cdf = "resc.ecdf", nboots = 0) summary(fit) #> Gaussian copula estimator: Yang et al. (2025) #> CDF transformation: resc.ecdf #>  #> Call: #> CopReg2sCOPE(formula = y ~ z_endog | x_exog + w_instr, data = sim_endog,  #>     cdf = "resc.ecdf", nboots = 0) #>  #> Coefficients: #>                Estimate #> `(Intercept)` 0.9909636 #> z_endog       1.4347301 #> x_exog        1.5132420 #> w_instr       0.3349117 #> z_endog_cop   0.7203308

what: Copula Regression: Hu et al. (2025) — CopReg2sCOPEnp
title: Copula Regression: Hu et al. (2025) — CopReg2sCOPEnp
text: Implements instrument-free two-stage copula control function estimator proposed Hu et al. (2025). exogenous regressors present, first stage estimates conditional distribution functions nonparametrically, requires suggested np package.

title: Copula Regression: Hu et al. (2025) — CopReg2sCOPEnp
code: CopReg2sCOPEnp(   formula,   data,   cdf = c("kde", "ecdf", "resc.ecdf", "adj.ecdf"),   nboots = 199 )

title: Copula Regression: Hu et al. (2025) — CopReg2sCOPEnp
text: formula two-part formula form y ~ endog1 + endog2 | exog1 + exog2, left-hand side lists endogenous regressors right-hand side bar lists exogenous regressors. Use - 1 remove intercept. data data.frame containing variables referenced formula. cdf Character string specifying cumulative distribution function estimator. One "kde", "ecdf", "resc.ecdf", "adj.ecdf". nboots Number bootstrap replications used compute standard errors. Set zero skip bootstrapping.

title: Copula Regression: Hu et al. (2025) — CopReg2sCOPEnp
text: object class endog_copula_fit: list components call matched call. method Character string identifying estimator. cdf CDF estimator used copula transformation. coefficients Matrix coefficient estimates Estimate column , nboots > 0, Std. Error column computed bootstrap replicates. residuals Numeric vector residuals structural part model (copula control terms excluded). bootstrap Matrix bootstrap coefficient replicates, NULL nboots = 0. Objects class support print(), summary(), coef(), residuals(), confint().

title: Copula Regression: Hu et al. (2025) — CopReg2sCOPEnp
text: Hu, X., Y. Qian, H. Xie (2025). Correcting endogeneity via instrument-free two-stage nonparametric copula control functions. National Bureau Economic Research Working Paper 33607.

title: Copula Regression: Hu et al. (2025) — CopReg2sCOPEnp
code: data(sim_endog)  # Without exogenous regressors the estimator does not need the 'np' package fit <- CopReg2sCOPEnp(y ~ z_endog, data = sim_endog,                       cdf = "resc.ecdf", nboots = 0) summary(fit) #> Gaussian copula estimator: Hu et al. (2025) #> CDF transformation: resc.ecdf #>  #> Call: #> CopReg2sCOPEnp(formula = y ~ z_endog, data = sim_endog, cdf = "resc.ecdf",  #>     nboots = 0) #>  #> Coefficients: #>                 Estimate #> `(Intercept)`  1.1946512 #> z_endog        2.1882798 #> z_endog_cop   -0.4709731  # \donttest{ # With exogenous regressors the nonparametric first stage uses 'np' if (requireNamespace("np", quietly = TRUE)) {   fit_np <- CopReg2sCOPEnp(y ~ z_endog | x_exog,                            data = sim_endog[1:150, ],                            cdf = "resc.ecdf", nboots = 0)   coef(fit_np) } #> `(Intercept)`       z_endog        x_exog   z_endog_cop  #>     0.9153671    -2.1121331     1.5464786     4.6185621  # }

what: Copula Regression: Breitung, Mayer, and Wied (2024) — CopRegBMW
title: Copula Regression: Breitung, Mayer, and Wied (2024) — CopRegBMW
text: Implements estimator proposed Breitung, Mayer, Wied (2024) combines classical first-stage regressions copula-based control functions. exogenous regressors, endogenous regressor first regressed exogenous ones copula control function built CDF-transformed (normal-quantile mapped) first-stage residuals. Without exogenous regressors CDF-transformed endogenous regressors enter control-function regression directly.

title: Copula Regression: Breitung, Mayer, and Wied (2024) — CopRegBMW
code: CopRegBMW(   formula,   data,   cdf = c("kde", "ecdf", "resc.ecdf", "adj.ecdf"),   nboots = 199 )

title: Copula Regression: Breitung, Mayer, and Wied (2024) — CopRegBMW
text: formula two-part formula form y ~ endog1 + endog2 | exog1 + exog2, left-hand side lists endogenous regressors right-hand side bar lists exogenous regressors. Use - 1 remove intercept. data data.frame containing variables referenced formula. cdf Character string specifying cumulative distribution function estimator. One "kde", "ecdf", "resc.ecdf", "adj.ecdf". nboots Number bootstrap replications used compute standard errors. Set zero skip bootstrapping.

title: Copula Regression: Breitung, Mayer, and Wied (2024) — CopRegBMW
text: object class endog_copula_fit: list components call matched call. method Character string identifying estimator. cdf CDF estimator used copula transformation. coefficients Matrix coefficient estimates Estimate column , nboots > 0, Std. Error column computed bootstrap replicates. residuals Numeric vector residuals structural part model (copula control terms excluded). bootstrap Matrix bootstrap coefficient replicates, NULL nboots = 0. Objects class support print(), summary(), coef(), residuals(), confint().

title: Copula Regression: Breitung, Mayer, and Wied (2024) — CopRegBMW
text: Breitung, J., . Mayer, D. Wied (2024). Asymptotic properties endogeneity corrections using nonlinear transformations. Econometrics Journal 27 (3), 362–383.

title: Copula Regression: Breitung, Mayer, and Wied (2024) — CopRegBMW
code: data(sim_endog)  fit <- CopRegBMW(y ~ z_endog | x_exog + w_instr, data = sim_endog,                  cdf = "resc.ecdf", nboots = 0) summary(fit) #> Gaussian copula estimator: Breitung, Mayer, and Wied (2024) #> CDF transformation: resc.ecdf #>  #> Call: #> CopRegBMW(formula = y ~ z_endog | x_exog + w_instr, data = sim_endog,  #>     cdf = "resc.ecdf", nboots = 0) #>  #> Coefficients: #>                 Estimate #> `(Intercept)`  0.9767925 #> z_endog        2.6199074 #> x_exog         1.5141439 #> w_instr       -0.3625772 #> z_endog_cop   -0.4798474

title: Bayesian Gaussian Copula Estimator (delegated) — CopRegBayes
text: Thin wrapper around endogCopulaBayes::CopRegBayes(). Requires optional endogCopulaBayes package installed.

title: Bayesian Gaussian Copula Estimator (delegated) — CopRegBayes
code: CopRegBayes(   data,   iterations = 10000,   burnin = 2000,   thin = 10,   startvalue = NULL )

title: Bayesian Gaussian Copula Estimator (delegated) — CopRegBayes
text: data Data frame containing columns y, z, x. iterations Total number MCMC iterations. burnin Number initial iterations discarded burn-. thin Thinning interval applied burn-. startvalue Optional numeric vector starting values.

title: Bayesian Gaussian Copula Estimator (delegated) — CopRegBayes
text: MCMC output returned endogCopulaBayes::CopRegBayes(), containing posterior draws model parameters burn-thinning. See documentation endogCopulaBayes package full structure.

title: Bayesian Gaussian Copula Estimator (delegated) — CopRegBayes
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Requires the optional 'endogCopulaBayes' package: data(sim_endog) bayes_data <- data.frame(y = sim_endog$y, z = sim_endog$z_endog,                          x = sim_endog$x_exog) draws <- CopRegBayes(bayes_data, iterations = 10000, burnin = 2000) } # }

Error column computed bootstrap replicates. residuals Numeric vector residuals structural part model (copula control terms excluded). bootstrap Matrix bootstrap coefficient replicates, NULL nboots = 0. text: Haschka, R. E. (2024). Robustness copula-correction models causal analysis: Exploiting -regressor correlation. IMA Journal Management Mathematics 36 (1), 161–180.

code: data(sim_endog)  fit <- CopRegIMA(y ~ z_endog | x_exog + w_instr, data = sim_endog,                  cdf = "resc.ecdf", nboots = 0) summary(fit) #> Gaussian copula estimator: Haschka (2024) #> CDF transformation: resc.ecdf #>  #> Call: #> CopRegIMA(formula = y ~ z_endog | x_exog + w_instr, data = sim_endog,  #>     cdf = "resc.ecdf", nboots = 0) #>  #> Coefficients: #>                Estimate #> `(Intercept)` 0.9909636 #> z_endog       1.4347301 #> x_exog        1.5132420 #> w_instr       0.3349117 #> z_endog_cop   0.7203308

Use - 1 remove intercept. data data.frame containing variables referenced formula. cdf Character string specifying cumulative distribution function estimator. text: Liengaard, B. D., J.-M. Becker, M. Bennedsen, P. Heiler, L. N. Taylor, C. M. Ringle (2025). Dealing regression models' endogeneity means adjusted estimator Gaussian copula approach. Journal Academy Marketing Science 53, 279–299.

title: Copula Regression: Liengaard et al. (2025) — CopRegJAMS
code: data(sim_endog)  fit <- CopRegJAMS(y ~ z_endog | x_exog + w_instr, data = sim_endog,                   cdf = "adj.ecdf", nboots = 0) summary(fit) #> Gaussian copula estimator: Liengaard et al. (2025) #> CDF transformation: adj.ecdf #>  #> Call: #> CopRegJAMS(formula = y ~ z_endog | x_exog + w_instr, data = sim_endog,  #>     cdf = "adj.ecdf", nboots = 0) #>  #> Coefficients: #>                  Estimate #> `(Intercept)`  0.97746548 #> z_endog        2.09084002 #> x_exog         1.51392827 #> w_instr       -0.05082422 #> z_endog_cop    0.01732337

title: Panel Gaussian Copula Estimator (delegated) — CopRegML_par
text: Thin wrapper around endogCopulaPanel::CopRegML_par(). function available optional endogCopulaPanel package installed.

title: Panel Gaussian Copula Estimator (delegated) — CopRegML_par
code: CopRegML_par(   formula,   index,   data,   ecdf = TRUE,   nboots = 199,   starting_values = NULL,   method = "Nelder-Mead" )

title: Panel Gaussian Copula Estimator (delegated) — CopRegML_par
text: formula Two-part formula y ~ endog | exog describing model. index Character vector length two panel time identifiers. data Data frame containing referenced variables. ecdf Logical; use empirical CDFs (TRUE) kernel estimates (FALSE). nboots Number bootstrap replications. starting_values Optional numeric vector starting values. method Optimisation method passed stats::optim().

title: Panel Gaussian Copula Estimator (delegated) — CopRegML_par
text: fitted panel model returned endogCopulaPanel::CopRegML_par(): maximum likelihood coefficient estimates fixed-effects copula panel model together bootstrap standard errors nboots > 0. See documentation endogCopulaPanel package full structure.

title: Panel Gaussian Copula Estimator (delegated) — CopRegML_par
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Requires the optional 'endogCopulaPanel' package and panel data with # numeric id and time variables: fit <- CopRegML_par(y ~ z_endog | x_exog + as.factor(year),                     index = c("id", "year"), data = panel_data,                     nboots = 199) } # }

what: Copula Regression: Park and Gupta (2012) — CopRegPG
title: Copula Regression: Park and Gupta (2012) — CopRegPG
text: Implements Gaussian copula approach proposed Park Gupta (2012) correcting endogeneity linear models. Bootstrap standard errors obtained via resampling.

title: Copula Regression: Park and Gupta (2012) — CopRegPG
code: CopRegPG(   formula,   data,   cdf = c("kde", "ecdf", "resc.ecdf", "adj.ecdf"),   nboots = 199 )

title: Copula Regression: Park and Gupta (2012) — CopRegPG
text: formula two-part formula form y ~ endog1 + endog2 | exog1 + exog2, left-hand side lists endogenous regressors right-hand side bar lists exogenous regressors. Use - 1 remove intercept. data data.frame containing variables referenced formula. cdf Character string specifying cumulative distribution function estimator. One "kde", "ecdf", "resc.ecdf", "adj.ecdf". nboots Number bootstrap replications used compute standard errors. Set zero skip bootstrapping.

title: Copula Regression: Park and Gupta (2012) — CopRegPG
text: object class endog_copula_fit: list components call matched call. method Character string identifying estimator. cdf CDF estimator used copula transformation. coefficients Matrix coefficient estimates Estimate column , nboots > 0, Std. Error column computed bootstrap replicates. residuals Numeric vector residuals structural part model (copula control terms excluded). bootstrap Matrix bootstrap coefficient replicates, NULL nboots = 0. Objects class support print(), summary(), coef(), residuals(), confint().

title: Copula Regression: Park and Gupta (2012) — CopRegPG
text: Point estimates numerically equivalent published reference implementation. Bootstrap standard errors deliberately differ: reference code omits inverse-normal (qnorm) transformation copula terms inside bootstrap replicates even though main fit applies . every replicate refits estimator main fit, standard errors describe estimator actually reported.

title: Copula Regression: Park and Gupta (2012) — CopRegPG
text: Park, S. S. Gupta (2012). Handling endogenous regressors joint estimation using copulas. Marketing Science 31 (4), 567–586.

title: Copula Regression: Park and Gupta (2012) — CopRegPG
code: data(sim_endog)  fit <- CopRegPG(y ~ z_endog | x_exog + w_instr, data = sim_endog,                 cdf = "resc.ecdf", nboots = 0) summary(fit) #> Gaussian copula estimator: Park and Gupta (2012) #> CDF transformation: resc.ecdf #>  #> Call: #> CopRegPG(formula = y ~ z_endog | x_exog + w_instr, data = sim_endog,  #>     cdf = "resc.ecdf", nboots = 0) #>  #> Coefficients: #>                  Estimate #> `(Intercept)`  1.00458029 #> z_endog        1.15532197 #> x_exog         1.51341797 #> w_instr       -0.05337007 #> z_endog_cop    1.02440912  if (requireNamespace("ks", quietly = TRUE)) {   fit_kde <- CopRegPG(y ~ z_endog | x_exog + w_instr, data = sim_endog,                       cdf = "kde", nboots = 0)   coef(fit_kde) } #> `(Intercept)`       z_endog        x_exog       w_instr   z_endog_cop  #>    1.01818468    1.01531666    1.51358948   -0.05371194    1.18281589

what: Confidence intervals for endog_copula_fit objects — confint.endog_copula_fit
title: Confidence intervals for endog_copula_fit objects — confint.endog_copula_fit
text: Computes confidence intervals estimated coefficients. default type = "normal" uses normal approximation based bootstrap standard errors; type = "percentile" uses percentiles bootstrap replicates stored object$bootstrap. require model fitted nboots > 0.

title: Confidence intervals for endog_copula_fit objects — confint.endog_copula_fit
code: # S3 method for class 'endog_copula_fit' confint(object, parm, level = 0.95, type = c("normal", "percentile"), ...)

title: Confidence intervals for endog_copula_fit objects — confint.endog_copula_fit
text: object object returned one copula estimators. parm Coefficients compute intervals , either names indices. Defaults coefficients. level Confidence level. Defaults 0.95. type Either "normal" (normal approximation bootstrap standard errors) "percentile" (percentile intervals bootstrap replicates). ... Currently unused.

title: Confidence intervals for endog_copula_fit objects — confint.endog_copula_fit
text: matrix one row per coefficient columns giving lower upper confidence limits, labelled corresponding percentiles. Coefficients without bootstrap information filled NA.

title: Confidence intervals for endog_copula_fit objects — confint.endog_copula_fit
code: data(sim_endog) fit <- CopRegPG(y ~ z_endog | x_exog + w_instr, data = sim_endog,                 cdf = "resc.ecdf", nboots = 25) #> Computing bootstrap standard errors (this may take a while)... confint(fit) #>                     2.5 %      97.5 % #> `(Intercept)`  0.94948822 1.059672360 #> z_endog       -0.01926163 2.329905570 #> x_exog         1.46391015 1.562925778 #> w_instr       -0.10831481 0.001574675 #> z_endog_cop   -0.24348216 2.292300395 confint(fit, parm = "z_endog", type = "percentile") #>             2.5 %  97.5 % #> z_endog 0.2974968 2.25039

what: endogCopula: Gaussian Copula Based Endogeneity Corrections — endogCopula-package
title: endogCopula: Gaussian Copula Based Endogeneity Corrections — endogCopula-package
text: endogCopula package implements collection copula-based estimators addressing endogeneity linear models. includes cross-sectional estimators Park Gupta (2012) (CopRegPG()), Yang et al. (2025) (CopReg2sCOPE()), Hu et al. (2025) (CopReg2sCOPEnp()), Breitung et al. (2024) (CopRegBMW()), Haschka (2024) (CopRegIMA()), Liengaard et al. (2025) (CopRegJAMS()).

title: endogCopula: Gaussian Copula Based Endogeneity Corrections — endogCopula-package
text: cross-sectional estimators share two-part formula interface y ~ endog1 + endog2 | exog1 + exog2, cdf argument selecting marginal CDF estimator ("kde", "ecdf", "resc.ecdf", "adj.ecdf"), bootstrap standard errors controlled via nboots. Fitted models returned endog_copula_fit objects print(), summary(), coef(), residuals(), confint() methods. panel estimator Haschka (2022) Bayesian sampler Haschka (2022b) live companion packages endogCopulaPanel endogCopulaBayes; wrappers CopRegML_par() CopRegBayes() delegate packages installed.

title: endogCopula: Gaussian Copula Based Endogeneity Corrections — endogCopula-package
text: Maintainer: Ashwin Malshe ashwin@malshe.com

what: Simulated Gaussian Copula Endogeneity Data — sim_endog
title: Simulated Gaussian Copula Endogeneity Data — sim_endog
text: synthetic dataset generated illustrate Gaussian copula endogeneity corrections. dependent variable driven endogenous regressor correlated structural error via shared noise component.

title: Simulated Gaussian Copula Endogeneity Data — sim_endog
code: sim_endog

title: Simulated Gaussian Copula Endogeneity Data — sim_endog
text: data frame 2000 rows 4 variables: y Outcome variable z_endog Endogenous regressor correlated error term x_exog Exogenous regressor w_instr Instrument used copula estimators

title: Simulated Gaussian Copula Endogeneity Data — sim_endog
text: true data-generating process $$y = 1 + 2 z_{\text{endog}} + 1.5 x_{\text{exog}} + u,$$ latent Gaussian factor induces correlation \(z_{\text{endog}}\) structural error \(u\). instrument \(w_{\text{instr}}\) shifts endogenous regressor excluded outcome equation.

title: Simulated Gaussian Copula Endogeneity Data — sim_endog

title: Summary method for endog_copula_fit — summary.endog_copula_fit
text: object class summary.endog_copula_fit: list carrying original call, method, cdf, coefficients, plus coefficient_table z statistics p-values (NULL bootstrap standard errors available), residuals, matrix bootstrap replicates, number bootstrap draws nboots.

title: Summary method for endog_copula_fit — summary.endog_copula_fit
code: data(sim_endog) fit <- CopRegPG(y ~ z_endog | x_exog + w_instr, data = sim_endog,                 cdf = "resc.ecdf", nboots = 0) summary(fit) #> Gaussian copula estimator: Park and Gupta (2012) #> CDF transformation: resc.ecdf #>  #> Call: #> CopRegPG(formula = y ~ z_endog | x_exog + w_instr, data = sim_endog,  #>     cdf = "resc.ecdf", nboots = 0) #>  #> Coefficients: #>                  Estimate #> `(Intercept)`  1.00458029 #> z_endog        1.15532197 #> x_exog         1.51341797 #> w_instr       -0.05337007 #> z_endog_cop    1.02440912
